ai_summary: Gillanders Arbuthnot & Co. Ltd. conducted its 92nd Annual General Meeting, where it presented financial statements for the year ended March 31, 2026, alongside Statutory and Secretarial Auditor's reports that contained no qualifications or adverse remarks. The key takeaway and a potential red flag involve the proposal to approve dividends on 7.75% Cumulative Redeemable Preference Shares (CRPS) for the *financial years 2018-19 and 2019-20*. This significant delay in fulfilling preference dividend obligations, now being addressed several years later, raises concerns about the company's historical liquidity or financial performance in those periods.
